name: Natural Park of the Desert de les Palmes — Les Fonts del Desert (circular PR-CV 451)
name_original: Parque Natural del Desert de les Palmes — Les Fonts del Desert (circular PR-CV 451)
region: Parc Natural del Desert de les Palmes
region_intro: Bus station hiking trails around Parc Natural del Desert de les Palmes offer access to a mountainous coastal park characterized by rugged summits and diverse Mediterranean flora. The region features ranges like Sierra del Desierto and Sierra de Les Santes, with the unique presence of fan palms. Pine forests and varied ecosystems provide a backdrop for trails, leading to panoramic views of the coast and surrounding areas. Best bus station hiking trails around Parc Natural del Desert de les Palmes The most popular bus station hiking route is Montornès Castle – Desert de les Palmes Pass loop from Montornés - Parreta, a 7.5 miles (12.1 km) trail that takes 4 hours 15 minutes to complete. This moderate route features significant elevation gain and historical points of interest. Another top favourite among local hikers is San Vicente Tower – Platja de l'Almadrava loop from Benicàssim, an easy 3.2 miles (5.2 km) path. This trail offers coastal views and passes by the historic San Vicente Tower. Local hikers also love the Albarrana Tower – Montornès Castle loop from Benicàssim, a 7.2 miles (11.6 km) trail leading through varied terrain with historical ruins, often completed in about 4 hours 5 minutes. Hiking around Parc Natural del Desert de les Palmes is defined by rugged summits, Mediterranean flora, and coastal views. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult trails, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Parc Natural del Desert de les Palmes are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 1,100 reviews. More than 8,700 hikers have used komoot to explore Parc Natural del Desert de les Palmes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many bus station hiking trails are available in Parc Natural del Desert de les Palmes?
A: There are over 180 hiking routes in Parc Natural del Desert de les Palmes that are accessible from bus stations. These trails offer a wide range of experiences, from easy strolls to more challenging ascents, ensuring options for various fitness levels.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike from bus stations in Parc Natural del Desert de les Palmes?
A: Spring and autumn are considered the ideal seasons for hiking in Parc Natural del Desert de les Palmes. During these times, temperatures are mild, and the natural beauty of the park is enhanced by blooming wildflowers or vibrant autumn foliage. Summer can be hot, so early mornings or late afternoons are recommended if hiking during this season.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes accessible from bus stations in the park?
A: Yes, many of the bus-accessible trails in Parc Natural del Desert de les Palmes are circular, allowing you to start and end your hike at the same point without needing to retrace your steps. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14838571" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montornès Castle – Desert de les Palmes Pass loop from Montornés - Parreta&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a moderate challenge.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect on bus-accessible trails in Parc Natural del Desert de les Palmes?
A: The park offers a diverse range of landscapes, even on bus-accessible routes. You'll encounter rugged mountain terrain, Mediterranean flora including maritime pines and the unique fan palms, and often breathtaking coastal views. Trails can lead through dense pine forests, open scrubland, and past rocky outcrops.

Q: What interesting sights can I see along bus-accessible routes?
A: Many trails offer glimpses of the park's rich history and natural beauty. You might pass by historical ruins like those of the ancient Carmelite monastery or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/669104" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miravet Castle&lt;/a&gt;. Natural highlights include impressive rock formations like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/674680" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Àgueda Needles Pass&lt;/a&gt; and various viewpoints offering panoramic vistas.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options from bus stations?
A: Absolutely. The park offers several easy and moderate routes suitable for families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31437152" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Vicente Tower – Platja de l'Almadrava loop from Benicàssim&lt;/a&gt; is an easy option, perfect for a shorter outing with children, offering coastal views and minimal elevation gain.

Q: Can I bring my dog on these bus-accessible trails?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on trails within Parc Natural del Desert de les Palmes, but it's always best to keep them on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near other hikers. Please ensure you clean up after your pet to help maintain the park's natural environment.

Q: What are the typical lengths and durations of bus-accessible hikes?
A: Hikes accessible from bus stations vary significantly in length and duration. You can find shorter, easy routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31437152" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Vicente Tower – Platja de l'Almadrava loop from Benicàssim&lt;/a&gt;, which is around 5 km and takes about 1.5 hours. Moderate trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/22047859" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albarrana Tower – Montornès Castle loop from Benicàssim&lt;/a&gt;, can be around 11.5 km and take 4-5 hours.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints accessible from bus station trails?
A: Yes, many bus-accessible trails lead to or pass by stunning viewpoints. The park's highest point, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1323360" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bartolo Summit&lt;/a&gt; (729m), offers spectacular panoramic views of the Benicàssim coast, the Columbretes Islands, and the Mediterranean Sea, and can be reached via various trails. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6410383" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Colomer Observatory&lt;/a&gt; also provides excellent vistas.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Parc Natural del Desert de les Palmes?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.58 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, the rewarding panoramic views from peaks like El Bartolo, and the tranquility found within the park's Mediterranean forests. The historical elements, such as the ancient monastery ruins, also add a unique dimension to the hiking experience.

Q: What should I wear for hiking in Parc Natural del Desert de les Palmes, especially in autumn?
A: In autumn, layers are key. Start with a moisture-wicking base layer, add a fleece or insulating mid-layer, and bring a waterproof and windproof outer shell, as weather can be unpredictable. Sturdy hiking boots with good ankle support are essential for the varied terrain. Don't forget sun protection, even in cooler months, and plenty of water.

Q: Are there options for shorter, easier hikes from bus stations?
A: Yes, if you're looking for a less strenuous outing, there are numerous easy routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31437152" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Vicente Tower – Platja de l'Almadrava loop from Benicàssim&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, offering a pleasant coastal walk without significant elevation, making it ideal for beginners or a quick stroll.
tips:
Hermitage San Miguel Arcángel. It is located on the summit of Pico Bartolo, at 728 meters. of altitude and dominating the entire area of the Desert of Las Palmas.
It can be accessed by a steep track, and from its location you can enjoy one of the most spectacular views. It is a pity that there is a real forest of radio antennas next to the hermitage, which together with the ancient and venerable temple offer a surreal image.

waypoints.name: Hermitage of Saint Michael on Pico Bartolo
